channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Cláudio Castro marca data para renunciar ao cargo; saiba quem assume o governo do RJ
item.description: Rio de Janeiro Governor Cláudio Castro will resign from office on Monday at 4:30 PM at Guanabara Palace, one day before the TSE resumes judgment that could render him ineligible for eight years. Without a vice governor, he will be replaced by Ricardo Couto, president of the TJ-RJ, who will call an indirect election in Alerj to choose the new governor. The resignation is a strategic move to nullify the TSE case and enable Castro to run for a Senate seat under the PL, with Douglas Ruas as the gubernatorial candidate.

item.title: Zema renuncia e critica governo Lula: “Somos um país roubado”
item.description: Governor Romeu Zema (Novo) resigned from office in Minas Gerais on Sunday (22), handing power to his vice-governor Mateus Simões (PSD). Zema, who is positioning himself as a pre-candidate for president in 2026, sharply criticized the Lula administration during the ceremony, claiming Brazil is being 'robbed' and that 'no one can tolerate the revelry anymore'. His resignation is part of a strategy to strengthen his national presidential bid.

item.title: INSS: CPMI vai pedir indiciamento de 200 pessoas em relatório oficial
item.description: The final report of the INSS CPI is expected to recommend the indictment of over 200 people, including members of the Lula government, spanning more than 5,000 pages prepared by rapporteur Alfredo Gaspar (União Brasil-AL). The document will be presented on March 25 and voted on March 26, the commission's last day, as government allies prepare an alternative 800-page report.

item.title: Zema deixa governo de Minas e abre caminho para corrida presidencial
item.description: Romeu Zema formally resigned as governor of Minas Gerais to dedicate himself fully to his presidential pre-campaign under the Novo party, anticipating an expected political move. His vice-governor, Mateus Simões, assumed the state executive in a ceremony at the ALMG and Palácio da Liberdade, positioning himself as a potential candidate for the Minas Gerais governorship in 2026. Zema ruled out alliances with figures like Flávio Bolsonaro or traditional right-wing parties, signaling an independent presidential bid.

item.title: Ministra Sônia Guajajara é internada em UTI em São Paulo
item.description: Indigenous Peoples Minister Sônia Guajajara (PSOL) was hospitalized in the ICU at InCor-HCFMUSP for investigation of an infectious condition, presenting high fever, abdominal pain, and malaise, with favorable clinical evolution according to an official statement. She announced she will leave office on March 30 to run for re-election as a federal deputy for São Paulo, with Eloy Terena expected to assume the ministry. Her case is being monitored by cardiologist Sérgio Timerman and infectologist Rinaldo Focaccia Siciliano.

item.title: Partido do chanceler alemăo lidera eleiçőes regionais; extrema direita avança
item.description: The conservative CDU party, led by German Chancellor Friedrich Merz, is leading the regional elections in Rhineland-Palatinate. The far-right may achieve a historic vote record, according to initial estimates released on Sunday (22).

item.title: Servidores protestam em MG: "Zema + Simões é igual trem desgovernado"
item.description: State public servants in Minas Gerais protested in Belo Horizonte against the government of Romeu Zema and Vice Governor Mateus Simões, labeling the administration a 'runaway train'. The demonstration coincided with Simões' inauguration as governor, following Zema's resignation to focus on a presidential pre-candidacy. Protesters denounced the deterioration of public services and lack of career valorization, with participation from teachers, health and environmental workers.

item.title: Flávio critica Lula e defende classificar facções criminosas como terroristas
item.description: In João Pessoa, Senator Flávio Bolsonaro, the PL's pré-candidate for president, criticized President Lula for alleged inaction against organized crime and said he would classify criminal factions as terrorist organizations if elected. He also dismissed claims of a possible U.S. intervention led by Trump against Lula's government as a lie and an attempt to spread terror due to the PT's incompetence.

item.title: Público canta parabéns para Michelle Bolsonaro, e Flávio exalta a ex-primeira-dama
item.description: During a pre-campaign event for Flávio Bolsonaro in João Pessoa, the public sang 'happy birthday' to Michelle Bolsonaro, who was also praised by the senator on social media. Flávio emphasized Michelle's role in the political landscape, downplaying the lack of support for his own presidential bid. The content includes links and excerpts from other UOL stories, but the central focus is the celebration of Michelle Bolsonaro's birthday.

item.title: Flávio: ladrăo vai mofar na cadeia e será neutralizado se enfrentar polícia
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro reiterated hardline security proposals, stating that criminals 'will rot in jail' and will be 'neutralized' if they confront police — a term associated with killing suspects in confrontations. He also advocated lowering the age of criminal responsibility to 16 (14 for rapists) and attacked Lula, accusing him of enabling crime and being soft on criminals. The remarks were made at rallies in João Pessoa and Natal, reinforcing a radical stance to gain public support amid perceptions of ineffective current policies.

item.title: Gleisi volta a criticar Selic e classifica taxa do rotativo do cartão de crédito de ‘criminosa’
item.description: Brazil's Minister of Institutional Relations, Gleisi Hoffmann (PT), criticized the Selic rate as 'shameful' and credit card revolving interest as 'criminal', arguing that high interest rates are undermining President Lula's gains in employment and income, burdening families and workers. She called for a 'civic crusade' against the current Selic level, describing the Central Bank's 0.25 percentage point cut as disappointing and insufficient amid global inflationary pressures.

item.title: PSDB lança ex-assessor do governo FHC para Câmara
item.description: The PSDB has nominated Raul Christiano, a former advisor to Fernando Henrique Cardoso’s government and one of the party’s founders, as its federal deputy candidate for São Paulo, targeting voters in the Baixada Santista region. The move aims to restore the party’s political relevance after a poor showing in the 2024 municipal elections, where it elected only two mayors in cities over 200,000 voters, and following the defection of six state deputies to the PSD.

item.title: Paes critica Cláudio Castro por deixar governo do Rio: “Governador omisso fugindo da justiça”
item.description: Eduardo Paes, a pre-candidate for Rio de Janeiro governor, criticized Governor Cláudio Castro for resigning one day before a TSE session that could render him ineligible, accusing him of being an 'omissive governor fleeing justice' and using a legal maneuver to evade responsibility for alleged crimes. Paes stated the resignation is an attempt to avoid ineligibility and that the TSE will not accept such tactics, noting Castro aims to name a successor to continue his political project.

item.title: Lula e Petro defendem fortalecimento do multilateralismo regional
item.description: Lula and Petro advocated for strengthening regional multilateralism during a meeting in Bogotá, focusing on the transition of Celac's presidency to Uruguay and promoting democracy against extremism. Lula also met with Burundi's president, celebrating the country's entry into the Global Alliance against Hunger and Poverty and highlighting Embrapa's expansion in Africa with a new unit in Ethiopia. There was a divergence on UN succession: Brazil supports Michelle Bachelet for the first Latin American female leadership, while Burundi backs Macky Sall.

item.title: Sônia Guajajara está internada em UTI no InCor, em SP, com suspeita de quadro infeccioso
item.description: Indigenous Peoples Minister Sônia Guajajara is hospitalized in the ICU at InCor in São Paulo for investigation of an infectious condition, with favorable clinical evolution and stable vital signs. She announced she will leave the ministry on March 30 to run for re-election as a federal deputy from São Paulo.

item.title: Flávio acena a Michelle em aniversário e tenta conter falta de apoio na pré-campanha
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) made a public gesture of support for former First Lady Michelle Bolsonaro on her birthday, aiming to quell rumors of distancing in the presidential pre-campaign. While allies consider her support likely, Michelle remains discreet, focused on family and accompanying Jair Bolsonaro, with no defined intensity of involvement. The PL views her as a strategic asset to improve performance among female voters but pressures her for a clearer stance as the electoral calendar advances.

item.title: Mateus Simões toma posse e promete dar continuidade a legado de Zema
item.description: Mateus Simões (PSD) was sworn in as the new governor of Minas Gerais on March 22, succeeding Romeu Zema (NOVO), and pledged to continue his predecessor's legacy, emphasizing the fight against organized crime, job creation, and interior development. During the ceremony, he stated he plans to visit 80 cities in the first 100 days of government and positioned himself as a pre-candidate for re-election in 2026, backed by União Brasil and the Progressistas party.

item.title: Zema renuncia ao governo de MG faz discurso em tom eleitoral: 'Brasil está sendo destruído'
item.description: Romeu Zema resigned as governor of Minas Gerais to focus on his 2026 presidential campaign, transferring power to his vice governor, Mateus Simões. In his farewell speech, he adopted an electoral tone and harshly criticized the Lula government, claiming 'Brazil is being destroyed.' His resignation marks the formal launch of his presidential bid.

item.title: Zema renuncia e faz discurso em tom eleitoral: ‘Brasil está sendo destruído’
item.description: Romeu Zema resigned as governor of Minas Gerais in a speech with electoral tone, criticizing the federal government and claiming Brazil is being destroyed by corruption and a 'surplus of thieves'. He declared himself a pre-candidate for president in 2026, though he is speculated as a potential running mate for right-wing tickets, and said it is time to 'change Brazil entirely'. His successor, Mateus Simões, assumed office and also criticized the federal government, citing structural issues with a bridge over the Grande River.

item.title: Flávio vai à Paraíba e fala em “levar esperança” ao Nordeste
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) visited João Pessoa to support the party affiliation of Efraim Filho, reinforcing the PL's strategy to expand its presence in the Northeast and structure state candidacies for 2026. He stated the group aims to 'bring hope' to the region, following his announcement in Natal of Álvaro Filho’s candidacy for Rio Grande do Norte governor. Efraim’s switch from União Brasil to PL came after the União-PP federation nominated Lucas Ribeiro as its gubernatorial candidate, making Efraim’s run within the former alliance unviable.

item.title: Luxemburgo tem alta hospitalar após diagnóstico de infecção no pulmão
item.description: Vanderlei Luxemburgo, former Brazil national team coach and Podemos’ Senate pre-candidate for Tocantins, was discharged from hospital after treatment for pulmonary infection with septic focus. He had been hospitalized in Palmas since Friday and will now continue recovery at home with respiratory physiotherapy and rest. Luxemburgo was announced as a pre-candidate one day before his hospitalization, during an event that also formalized the affiliations of Sandoval Cardoso and Osiris Damaso.

item.title: Na despedida, Zema ataca governo Lula: "Somos um país roubado"
item.description: Romeu Zema, former governor of Minas Gerais and presidential pre-candidate, harshly criticized the Lula government during the inauguration ceremony in Belo Horizonte, accusing Brazil of being 'robbed' by a corrupt system and claiming the country is being destroyed by the current administration. He highlighted his achievements in Minas Gerais, advocated for a country where the law applies equally to all, and called for an end to the 'untouchables' Brazil, directly targeting the STF and politicians involved in corruption schemes.

item.title: Castro renuncia ao governo do Rio às vésperas de julgamento no TSE
item.description: Rio de Janeiro Governor Cláudio Castro resigned on Monday, the day before the TSE was set to resume a trial that could render him ineligible for eight years over allegations of political and economic abuse during his re-election campaign. His resignation aims to preserve his eligibility for a 2026 Senate run, while interim governance falls to the president of the Rio de Janeiro Court of Justice, Ricardo Couto, who must call an indirect election in the state legislature to choose a temporary governor. Prior to stepping down, Castro dismissed 11 secretaries in key areas such as Civil Police, Infrastructure, and Tourism, framing the moves as part of the electoral calendar.

item.title: Valdemar homenageia Michelle por aniversário: “Mulher de força”
item.description: PL president Valdemar Costa Neto honored Michelle Bolsonaro on her 44th birthday, calling her a 'woman of faith, strength and purpose' and a 'phenomenon of Brazilian politics'. He praised her natural communication gift and said Brazil and the PL need her leadership. Carlos Bolsonaro also voiced support.

item.title: Flávio usa camiseta “Nordeste é solução” no Rio Grande do Norte
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) launched a pre-campaign tour in the Northeast, appearing in Natal (RN) wearing a 'Northeast is the solution' shirt, where he criticized Lula for allegedly releasing criminals and opposing the designation of gangs as terrorists. The event featured former Natal mayor Álvaro Dias (PL), who is running for governor of Rio Grande do Norte.

item.title: Bolsonaro cumpre todos os requisitos para estar em casa, diz Flávio
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) claims that former President Jair Bolsonaro meets all legal requirements for humanitarian house arrest due to health reasons, citing his age, comorbidities, and clinical deterioration linked to custodial conditions. He and lawyer Paulo Cunha Bueno submitted the request to the judiciary, arguing the measure is a legal obligation that must be upheld. Although the latest medical bulletin notes favorable clinical evolution, the ex-president remains in the ICU at DF Star Hospital in Brasília with bilateral bacterial pneumonia, without a forecast for discharge.

item.title: O aceno de Flávio Bolsonaro à candidatura de Michelle no DF
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro signaled support for Michelle Bolsonaro's Senate candidacy in the Federal District during a pre-campaign event in João Pessoa, emphasizing her importance for the right to win the local election. He described her as a 'great cadre' built 'from the rib of Bolsonaro,' referencing the political legacy of his father, Jair Bolsonaro. The statement comes amid rumors of Michelle's discomfort with Flávio's selection as the PL's presidential pre-candidate, while she remains inactive in the campaign due to Jair Bolsonaro's hospitalization in Brasília.

item.title: Cláudio Castro marca para amanhã renúncia ao cargo de governador do RJ
item.description: Rio de Janeiro Governor Cláudio Castro (PL) announced he will resign from office tomorrow, on the eve of the resumption of the TSE trial that could render him ineligible for eight years. The move comes amid mounting pressure over his ongoing eligibility cases, appearing to be a preemptive step to mitigate electoral consequences ahead of the judicial verdict.

item.title: Lula defende integração regional e uso soberano de minerais críticos
item.description: President Lula advocated for Latin American and Caribbean countries to participate in all stages of the critical minerals value chain—from extraction to final product—to promote internal development and prevent other nations from enriching themselves at the region’s expense. He emphasized regional integration, strengthening Mercosul and Celac, and expanding intraregional trade as tools to boost bargaining power with global investors and counter geopolitical instability.

item.title: Em pré-campanha, Flávio Bolsonaro adota tom leve e mira ‘cansaço’ do eleitor com Lula
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) is launching his 2026 pre-campaign with a softer tone to avoid democracy-risk rhetoric and appeal to centrist voters, aiming to reduce his current ~45% rejection rate. His strategy centers on highlighting voter fatigue with Lula’s government, with allies framing the fourth PT term as expired and ‘more of the same,’ while Flávio avoids direct attacks and focuses on technical, palatable messaging to broaden his appeal beyond the ideological right.

item.title: Cracolândia vai virar tema da disputa entre Tarcísio e Haddad em São Paulo
item.description: Tarcísio de Freitas plans to attack Fernando Haddad's campaign by reviving the Cracolândia issue, claiming Haddad's 'De Braços Abertos' program was ineffective and dubbed 'bolsa crack'. Although Haddad temporarily reduced user flow and crime in the Luz area, Tarcísio aims to use this as a weakness in the 2026 São Paulo gubernatorial race. Current public violence under Tarcísio's administration is also expected to be raised by the Workers' Party as a counterpoint.

item.title: Haddad não queria, mas vai ‘mergulhar’ na campanha por governo de SP, diz aliado
item.description: State deputy Emídio de Souza (PT-SP) said that, despite Fernando Haddad (PT) initially stating he would not run in 2026, he is being persuaded by Lula and party allies to 'dive into' the São Paulo gubernatorial race. Haddad, according to Souza, is in a stronger position than in 2022 when he lost to Tarcísio de Freitas in the runoff, and will contrast his platform with the governor’s record on privatization, security, and gender-based violence. The pre-candidacy was announced on April 19 in São Bernardo do Campo.

item.title: Pré-candidato ao Planalto, Zema renuncia em MG, e Simões assume o governo
item.description: Romeu Zema resigned as governor of Minas Gerais to focus on his presidential pre-candidacy. His vice, Mateus Simões de Almeida, assumed office and pledged balance between powers, criticizing the Lula government and the Mais Médicos program. Simões also announced infrastructure works on a federal bridge, blaming São Paulo’s government for the costs.

item.title: Zema oficializa renúncia ao governo de Minas
item.description: Minas Gerais Governor Romeu Zema (Novo) officially resigned from office on Sunday, the 22nd, to focus on his pre-campaign for the presidency. He transferred power to his vice-governor, Mateus Simões (PSD), complying with the legal deadline for disengagement before the 2026 elections. The move allows Zema to prepare for a presidential run, while Simões assumes state leadership until the end of the term.

item.title: La política boliviana se atomiza tras el derrumbe del MAS: récord de candidatos en las elecciones regionales
item.description: Bolivia records a historic high of over 32,000 candidates in Sunday’s regional elections, with 7.9 million voters eligible to choose governors, mayors, and local representatives. The surge reflects the fragmentation of the political landscape following the decline of MAS, which ruled for 20 years before losing the August presidential elections. Both the left and the centroderecha government of President Rodrigo Paz failed to unify their regional efforts under a single party or coalition.

item.title: No aguardado ano da colheita, Lula lida com uma safra indigesta
item.description: Lula faces significant challenges in his bid for re-election in 2026, with government disapproval rising and public perception of the economy worsening, despite official indicators showing improvement. Scandals involving his son, Lulinha, and attempts to link him to the Banco Master case, combined with rising fuel prices due to Middle East conflicts, have eroded his popularity. Second-round simulations show a technical tie with Flávio Bolsonaro, reflecting a highly polarized and uncertain electoral landscape.

item.title: França realiza 2º turno de disputadas eleições municipais
item.description: France holds the second round of municipal elections amid deep political polarization and record abstention. In Paris, the left led by Emmanuel Grégoire seeks to retain power after 25 years, while the far right aims to gain ground in the southeast, signaling its growing influence ahead of the 2027 presidential election.

item.title: Eleições municipais na França: participação nas primeiras horas de votação tem ligeiro neste 2° turno
item.description: Approximately 20.33% of French voters had cast their ballots by noon on Sunday (22) in the second round of municipal elections across over 1,500 municipalities. Turnout was described as slightly below initial expectations, indicating moderate voter engagement. The figures were released by France's Ministry of the Interior.

item.title: As estrelas da política que já estão confirmadas em filiação de Moro ao PL
item.description: Senator Sergio Moro will join the PL on Tuesday in a move led by Valdemar Costa Neto, with expected participation from Flávio Bolsonaro, who aims to use Paraná as a base for his presidential campaign. Moro’s shift follows his departure from União Brasil due to lack of space to run for Paraná governor, and the likely Senate candidates on his ticket are Deltan Dallagnol (Novo) and Filipe Barros (PL).

item.title: Os 7% que podem inclinar a balança (por Gaudêncio Torquato)
item.description: With 196 days until the 2026 presidential election, approximately 7% of Brazil's electorate identifies as independent, forming a decisive bloc in a context of intense polarization and technical ties between Lula and Flávio Bolsonaro. This voter, not necessarily ideologically moderate, is pragmatic and responsive to the economy, health, security, and ethics, basing their vote on governmental performance rather than party loyalty.

item.title: Quem é Mateus Simões, novo governador de Minas Gerais e herdeiro político de Zema
item.description: Mateus Simões, the vice-governor of Minas Gerais, assumes the governorship following the resignation of Romeu Zema, who is running for President of Brazil in 2026. The inauguration takes place this Sunday (22) at the Minas Gerais Legislative Assembly, followed by the official transfer of power at the Palácio da Liberdade. A member of the PSD, Simões is portrayed as Zema’s political heir, with a career in state public administration and a key role as an articulator of the current administration.

item.title: Mesmo sem receber apoio, Flávio afaga Michelle no dia do aniversário dela
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) congratulated Michelle Bolsonaro on social media for her birthday, despite her lack of engagement in his presidential pre-campaign. The gesture was seen as a personal affirmation, detached from the political context of mutual support. Michelle’s limited involvement in Flávio’s campaign has been noted in right-wing political circles.

item.title: Lula tem que ‘mostrar o que tem feito’ para vencer Flávio em 2026, diz deputado do PT
item.description: State deputy Emídio de Souza (PT-SP) said President Lula must showcase his accomplishments from his third term to defeat Flávio Bolsonaro in the 2026 elections. He emphasized that the government needs to effectively communicate its achievements, such as the income tax exemption for those earning up to R$ 5,000 monthly, while the opposition has not presented an alternative program. Souza also noted that Brazil’s presidential race has become highly polarized and tightly contested, resembling the U.S. two-party dynamic, where small details can decisively influence the outcome.

item.title: Itália vai às urnas neste domingo em 1º dia de referendo
item.description: Italy opened polling stations for the first day of a referendum on a judicial reform proposed by Giorgia Meloni's government. Voting continues on Monday, with over 51 million eligible voters, including citizens abroad. Turnout so far stands at 14%, with the highest participation in northern regions, particularly Emilia-Romagna.

item.title: Indicação de Messias ao STF só tem 25 apoios declarados no Senado
item.description: The nomination of Jorge Messias to the Supreme Federal Court has only 25 declared supporters in the Senate, falling short of the 41 votes needed. Before a full Senate vote, he must be approved by the Constitution and Justice Committee (CCJ), where he has 10 of the required 14 votes, with 7 opposed and 10 undeclared. Lula is working to build political backing, including potentially nominating Rodrigo Pacheco for Minas Gerais governor, despite Pacheco having been previously backed by Senate President Davi Alcolumbre for the STF seat.

item.title: Saiba quais senadores dizem apoiar Messias no STF
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva has nominated Jorge Messias to the STF, with 25 senators declaring support, leaving 16 more votes needed to reach the 41 required. Before the full Senate vote, Messias must be approved by the CCJ, where he already has 10 of the 14 required votes. Of the 81 senators surveyed by Poder360, 14 oppose, 39 have not declared a position, and 3 did not respond.

item.title: Saiba quais senadores dizem ser contra Messias no STF
item.description: Lula's nominee to the STF, Jorge Messias, has declared support from 25 senators but needs 16 more votes to reach the 41 required for approval. In the CCJ, he has 10 of the 14 needed votes, with 7 senators opposed and 10 undecided. Of the 81 senators surveyed by Poder360, 14 declared opposition to Messias, 39 did not state a position, and 3 did not respond.

item.title: Itália vota em referendo uma controversa reforma judiciária
item.description: Italians are voting in a referendum on a judicial reform proposed by Giorgia Meloni's government, which seeks to separate the careers of judges and prosecutors and reform the Superior Council of the Magistracy. The government claims the reform will make the judiciary more meritocratic and effective, while opponents denounce it as an attack on judicial independence and a political maneuver. The vote, taking place on March 22-23, may become a referendum on Meloni's leadership ahead of the 2027 legislative elections.

item.title: Kim Kataguiri diz que Moraes tem que apodrecer na cadeia
item.description: Federal deputy Kim Kataguiri delivered a confrontational speech at an MBL-organized anti-corruption march on Paulista Avenue, demanding that Supreme Court ministers Alexandre de Moraes and Dias Toffoli 'rot in prison'. He linked the ministers to alleged misconduct involving banker Daniel Vorcaro and claimed any legal action against him stems from comments about Senator Weverton Rocha, not corruption charges. Kataguiri said he would accept imprisonment as a matter of principle and accused the Lula government and STF of weaponizing state institutions against opponents.

item.title: Pesquisas eleitorais: o que dizem os últimos números da disputa entre Lula e Flávio
item.description: Recent polls show a technical tie between Lula (47.4%) and Flávio Bolsonaro (45.3%) in the 2026 presidential race, within a 2.5-point margin of error. Bolsonaro (PL) is gaining momentum while Lula struggles with negative government evaluations on inflation, security, and spending. Both candidates face high rejection rates (~55-56%), and no third-way candidate exceeds 10% support in a highly polarized environment.
